title: Lophiolepis ciliata subsp. szovitsii
what_description: Lophiolepis ciliata subsp. szovitsii is a plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae, native to the Caucasus region. It is an annual herb, with a height of 10-30 cm, and has a branched stem, bearing ovate-lanceolate leaves that are 2-6 cm long and ciliate along the margins. The inflorescence is a capitulum, consisting of 10-20 florets, with ligulate flowers in shades of yellow, and achenes that are 3-4 mm long and ciliate along the margins. The plant grows in rocky areas and dry slopes, typically in well-drained soil. Its distribution is limited to the northern and western parts of the Caucasus region.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Lophiolepis ciliata subsp. szovitsii are inflammation, which is reduced due to its anti-inflammatory properties, and diarrhea, which is alleviated by its ability to regulate bowel movements and stabilize the gut microbiome. Lophiolepis ciliata subsp. szovitsii also helps to prevent diabetes complications by lowering blood sugar levels and improving insulin sensitivity. Its anti-asthmatic properties enable it to alleviate respiratory issues such as asthma, and its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties help to relieve rheumatism. Additionally, Lophiolepis ciliata subsp. szovitsii has been shown to alleviate gastritis, hypertension, fever, wound healing, digestive issues, cough, urinary tract infections, stomach pain, and eczema. Furthermore, its antioxidant and anti-cancer properties help to prevent cancer, and its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties aid in the treatment of gastroenteritis, gastrointestinal issues, skin infections, and arthritis.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Lophiolepis ciliata subsp. szovitsii are attributed to its antioxidant activity, which is primarily due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. This plant exhibits antimicrobial properties, particularly against certain bacteria and fungi, which is attributed to the bioactive compounds such as volatile oils and terpenes. Its anti-inflammatory properties are associated with the saponins and phenolic compounds, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. Additionally, the plant has been found to possess analgesic activity, which is thought to be mediated by the flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. Furthermore, Lophiolepis ciliata subsp. szovitsii has also been shown to exhibit antifungal properties, which is attributed to the presence of terpenes and other bioactive compounds.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Lophiolepis ciliata subsp. szovitsii are flavonoids, including luteolin, kaempferol and quercetin, which exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, flavonoids also showed antidiabetic and antihepatotoxic activities. The presence of tannins, specifically gallic acid and ellagic acid, contributes to the plant's antimicrobial and antiviral properties. Phenolic acids such as ferulic and sinapic acids also exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. Saponins present in Lophiolepis ciliata subsp. szovitsii, including oleanolic acid and ursolic acid, display anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory activities. Furthermore, terpenoids including kaurenoic acid and 2-oxo-olean-12-en-3β-ol exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Lophiolepis ciliata subsp. szovitsii are infusion, decoction, tincture, salve and tea which are derived from its leaves and bark that are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids. Infusion is used for digestive issues due to its anti-inflammatory properties, decoction is employed for its antioxidant activity in treating various health conditions. Tincture is prepared by soaking the plant material in a solvent to create a concentrated extract used for its antimicrobial properties, salve is applied topically for wound healing and skin issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Tea made from the leaves is consumed for its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects in treating various health conditions. The preparations are used due to the plant's ability to modulate immune response and exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Lophiolepis ciliata subsp. szovitsii improperly include inducing nausea and vomiting due to its high saponin content, causing liver damage and potentially leading to liver failure as saponins can accumulate in the liver and cause cell damage, increasing the risk of bleeding due to its effect on blood platelets and coagulation factors, and experiencing allergic reactions such as hives, itching, and difficulty breathing as some individuals may be sensitive to its compounds. This is because Lophiolepis ciliata subsp. szovitsii contains bioactive compounds like saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids that can have toxic effects if not used properly. The improper use of Lophiolepis ciliata subsp. szovitsii can also lead to other health problems such as gastrointestinal issues, headaches, and dizziness. Individuals with pre-existing medical conditions, such as liver disease or bleeding disorders, may be more susceptible to these side effects. It is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using Lophiolepis ciliata subsp. szovitsii or any other herbal remedy.
